extra.reasoning:
The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" is VIOLATED. The vulnerability is the [t_resume_glitch] transition (line 43): on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 10): sent := true. This sets sent=true while opted_out is true, directly breaching the invariant. The normal [t_send_allowed] requires not opted_out, and [t_send_blocked] keeps sent=false when opted_out and stage<10, but once stage reaches 10 the glitch lets an opted-out send go through.

Advancing combination (each adv guard checks only stage, not opted_out, so opting out first does not block advancement): stage 0->1 key_0, 1->2 key_1, 2->3 key_2, 3->4 key_1, 4->5 key_2, 5->6 key_1, 6->7 key_1, 7->8 key_2, 8->9 key_0, 9->10 key_1. The wrong keys at any stage reset to stage 0 (e.g. key_1/key_2 at stage 0), but the witness uses only correct keys.

Trace of witness (12 events, within the 13-event bound):
init: opted_out=F, sent=F, stage=0
optout (t_optout): opted_out=T, sent=F, stage=0
key_0 (adv_0): stage=1
key_1 (adv_1): stage=2
key_2 (adv_2): stage=3
key_1 (adv_3): stage=4
key_2 (adv_4): stage=5
key_1 (adv_5): stage=6
key_1 (adv_6): stage=7
key_2 (adv_7): stage=8
key_0 (adv_8): stage=9
key_1 (adv_9): stage=10
send_attempt: opted_out=T and stage>=10 -> t_resume_glitch fires -> sent=T
Final: opted_out=T AND sent=T => invariant violated.
